source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Bestow Product organization is responsible for defining, designing, and delivering exceptional digital experiences that solve real problems. Our team collaborates across Engineering, Revenue and Ops teams to transform ideas into intuitive, valuable solutions that delight our users. We believe in evidence-based decision making and maintaining a relentless focus on customer outcomes to drive product excellence. We're building at the intersection of insurance infrastructure and AI-native product development. The best candidates will come with a working relationship with AI tools already established — and a hunger to push that further.

The role is remote or can be a hybrid role from our Dallas headquarters. #LI-Remote




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Bestow is looking for an exceptional Senior Product Manager who thrives on transforming complex challenges into elegant solutions. We're seeking a pioneer who can see beyond the immediate horizon – someone who combines strategic vision with tactical excellence to drive meaningful impact across their product area.

This role is perfect for a product leader who understands that the most meaningful innovations often arise from the most perplexing problems. You'll be the driving force behind multiple high-impact initiatives, translating business objectives and customer needs into products that truly make a difference. Your ability to navigate ambiguity, inspire cross-functional teams, and deliver measurable results will be instrumental in advancing our mission to increase financial stability for everyone.

As a Senior Product Manager at Bestow, you will:

 - Lead complex product initiatives across your area

 - Leverage AI tools and agentic workflows as a core part of your product development process — from discovery and synthesis to roadmap communication and stakeholder prep

 - Design products and features with AI-assisted experiences in mind, understanding how agents, automation, and intelligent systems change user expectations

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate customer needs and business objectives into compelling product features and experiences

 - Develop and maintain product roadmaps that align with company strategy, balancing short-term wins with long-term vision

 - Define success metrics and build data-driven feedback loops to measure product performance and inform future decisions

 - Model AI-native working habits for the broader team and contribute to Bestow's shared knowledge base of tools, skills, and workflows

 - Identify opportunities for product improvements through customer research, market analysis, and competitive intelligence

 - Partner with Engineering, Design, Marketing, and Operations to ensure successful pr

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager (Fullstack)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.



ABOUT THE TEAM

The Pre-Sales module team owns the agent-facing quote and illustration tools that power Bestow's life insurance offerings. We work across the full stack to deliver the end-to-end pre-sales journey from quote generation through illustration management, partnering closely with product and design to ship features that drive agent adoption and conversion. The team operates in a fast-moving, high-stakes domain where quality and velocity both matter.

Within Bestow Engineering, our major functional teams are product engineering, infrastructure, IT, data and analytics, martech, and program management. Frontend Platform works closely with upstream engineering teams, experience design, and product management to define and prioritize the product roadmap.

We are looking for a Senior Engineering Manager to join our team.

This role will report to our Director of Engineering and is open to Remote (US).

#LI-REMOTE



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Work directly with other engineering teams, product, design, TPM, and external stakeholders to introduce new customer-facing platform features and support/evolve existing products.

 - Work with a distributed team of engineers–mentoring and driving engineering best practices.

 - Assist in setting the roadmap for team deliverables in a dynamic environment.

 - Drive delivery of high-quality product initiatives and engineering improvements.

 - Work with the team to identify, scope, and schedule improvements to the frontend platform.

 - Communicate effectively, excelling at explaining complex systems and requirements to non-technical cross-functional partners.

 - Be very technical and willing to participate in system design, code reviews, incident investigations, and directly contribute code towards team initiatives.

 - Be data-driven in their engineering management practices, fostering continuous improvement.



WHO YOU ARE

 - 8+ years experience developing software, with at least 3 years of engineering management experience, including managing teams of 8+ engineers.

 - Proficient across the full stack — frontend (React, Remix/Next.js), backend services (Node, GoLang, and/or Python), APIs, and relational databases.

 - Deep understanding of the entire software development lifecycle, including feature inception, GenAI developer tooling, continuous delivery, and release strategies.

 - Ability to define and track key metrics, drive accountability across teams, and consistently meet established Service Level Agreements (SLAs).

 - Ability to understand complex, cross-team technical problems, identify systemic areas of risk, and drive resolution at an organizational level.

 - Ability to 'go deep' technically when necessary. You should be able to contribute changes to

source_jobs.title: Staff Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The IPC (Implementation & Platform Configuration) Engineering team powers the digital life insurance enrollment experience that Bestow's carrier partners rely on. You'll work alongside Product Managers, Underwriters, and external partners to build and operate the modular services — written in Python, Go, and Java — that sit at the core of Bestow's platform. The team integrates across Bestow's service landscape using gRPC, REST, and async messaging, all running on Kubernetes in Google Cloud Platform. As a Staff Software Engineer, you'll set technical direction for the team, drive architectural decisions, and own the reliability and scalability of IPC's systems as Bestow onboards new carrier partners.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the technical architecture for IPC services, making design decisions that balance speed of delivery with long-term maintainability

 - Design, build, and operate backend services using Python + PostgreSQL, Go, and a Java-based Drools rules engine

 - Lead cross-team technical initiatives — coordinate with other Bestow engineering teams to plan and execute integration work across the platform

 - Drive platform capabilities that scale with Bestow's growing partner ecosystem — new carrier onboardings, product launches, and configuration flexibility

 - Mentor engineers on the team through code review, design review, and pairing — raise the technical bar without bottlenecking delivery

 - Ensure production reliability: define SLOs, improve observability, and share on-call responsibility for IPC workloads

 - Use agentic AI coding tools as a core part of your workflow and help the team adopt effective AI-assisted development practices

 - Communicate technical decisions, trade-offs, and status clearly in writing — the team is remote-first and async-heavy




WHO YOU ARE

 - 8+ years of professional backend software development experience; you've operated at the Staff or Senior level before

 - Strong proficiency in Go and Python; Java experience is a plus

 - Deep experience with distributed systems, microservices, and API design (Protocol Buffers, OpenAPI)

 - Comfortable with PostgreSQL or other relational databases at scale

 - Hands-on with cloud infrastructure — Kubernetes, Docker, GCP (or equivalent)

 - Proven track record building, deploying, and troubleshooting production services end-to-end

 - You seek out problems before they find you — proactively identifying risks, proposing solutions, and driving them to completion

 - You write maintainable, well-tested software and hold yourself and your peers to a high quality bar

 - Demonstrated experience with agentic AI coding tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) integrated into your daily workflow — not occas

source_jobs.title: VP, Information Technology
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are at an exciting inflection point. As we scale our platform and deepen our footprint across the enterprise, technology is no longer just a support function. It's a core driver of how we compete, operate, and grow. We're looking for a Vice President of Information Technology to lead that charge.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a high-visibility, high-impact executive role where you will own the full technology infrastructure, business technology, and IT strategy for a fast-growing SaaS company. You will sit at the intersection of business and technology, partnering directly with C-suite leaders and functional heads to ensure our systems, processes, and people are built to scale.



Our Environment

This is not a traditional IT operation. We run no on-premise servers. Our entire infrastructure is cloud-based, and our predominantly remote workforce operates on Macs. Our core collaboration stack includes Google Workspace, Zoom, Slack, and Claude. We are a technology-forward company that expects IT to operate the same way: modern, automated, and continuously improving.

You'll inherit a solid foundation and a capable team of 8. Things work today, but we need to prepare for the next phase. Your job is to bring the executive leadership, process maturity, and strategic vision needed to evolve IT into a world-class function that grows alongside the business.

If you thrive in environments where the problems are complex, the pace is fast, and the stakes are high, this role was built for you.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Technology Strategy & Roadmap

 - Define and execute a multi-year IT strategy aligned to company growth objectives, with a focus on scalability, security, and operational excellence.

 - Serve as a key member of the senior leadership team, advising the CTO and functional leaders on technology investments, risks, and opportunities.

 - Build and maintain a forward-looking technology roadmap that anticipates business needs and keeps the organization ahead of the curve.

Business Technology & Applications

 - Own the strategy, administration, and optimization of enterprise business systems including CRM (Salesforce), telephony and contact center platforms, productivity suites, and other business-critical SaaS applications.

 - Partner with functional leaders across Sales, Customer Success, Finance, and Operations to ensure business systems support their workflows, reporting needs, and growth objectives.

 - Lead the evaluation and selection of a next-generation telephony and contact center platform, with a focus on AI-native capabilities, automation, and modern customer engagement workflows.

 - Manage vendor strategy, contract negotiations, and lifecycle management for major thi

source_jobs.title: Payments Product Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Bestow Product team is responsible for defining, designing, and delivering exceptional experiences for our customers. Our team collaborates across Engineering, Design, Revenue and Ops teams to transform product opportunities into solutions that drive revenue.

This is a brand-new role on the product team. Your experience will help shape what it becomes. You'll own payments excellence by investigating patterns, building business cases, and directly impacting customer revenue and operational efficiency.

This role reports to our Director of Product Management and is open to Remote (US). #LIRemote




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Bestow is looking for a Payments Product Analyst to contribute in the following ways:

 - Own the investigation lifecycle. Take a signal (high NSF rate, reconciliation gap, auth rate drop) from definition through root-cause analysis to a written recommendation, coordinating with analytics, revenue, and support teams along the way.

 - Connect data and context: Frame the right questions with our analytics team and write your own SQL to dig into payment data, then pair the numbers with qualitative context from revenue and support that data alone won't surface. Work directly with carrier partner teams to understand the full picture.

 - Go deep on every opportunity. Independently analyze each opportunity’s revenue impact, root cause, urgency. Use AI tooling and your own judgment to move quickly without sacrificing quality.

 - Produce intelligence artifacts. Your output is a document covering the problem, why it's happening, what we should do, and what it's worth.

 - Partner with the product team. Translate your findings into clearly prioritized opportunities so product managers can act on them. Help maintain and shape the opportunity backlog.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Payments-fluent. You know what an ACH return code means, why a reconciliation breaks, how retry logic works, and what a chargeback lifecycle looks like.

 - Investigative by nature. You dig into discrepancies and trace issues across complex, multi-party payment flows. Messy data doesn't scare you.

 - A clear communicator. You're comfortable owning external relationships with partner’s finance teams and coordinating across internal stakeholders.

 - Process-minded. You see manual work and immediately think about how to systematize and document it. You turn fragmented data into executive-ready views.

 - Experienced. You bring 3+ years in payment analysis, payment operations, or financial reconciliation ideally at a payments company, SaaS platform, or fintech.

 - SQL-proficient. You write your own queries to pull, join, and analyze payment data for research and root-cause work—without waiting on someone else to get th

source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.



ABOUT THE TEAM

Bestow's Revenue team drives growth for our enterprise SaaS platform, the technology carriers like Nationwide, Transamerica, and USAA rely on to modernize life insurance. Sales Operations sits at the center of that engine: turning raw pipeline data into a clear picture of what's working and what we can do to drive continued growth. This role reports directly to the CRO.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the revenue operating rhythm: build and maintain ELT materials, run bi-weekly pipeline reviews, and produce weekly revenue reports with week-over-week trend analysis

 - Manage Salesforce as the source of truth: build reports and dashboards, track pipeline velocity, and flag stalled accounts, slipping close dates, and declining activity before they become surprises

 - Translate data into insights: create analysis using our structured data and synthesize reporting into a clear narrative and recommendation for the CRO and sales leadership

 - Own the full sales cadence: preview decks before major calls, keep teams accountable to update materials on time, and ensure the revenue org runs without gaps

 - Lead go-to-market research on a monthly/quarterly basis: conduct TAM analysis for new market motions, segment prospects, and map who's already been contacted against new ideal customer profiles

 - Build client dossiers ahead of strategic meetings: structured research packets on key accounts (especially publicly traded carriers) to support sales leaders in high-impact interactions

 - Iterate on pricing models with Finance: develop a rigorous framework for annuities and life insurance pricing, including benchmarking against market data

 - Build a consolidated forecast view: combine pipeline data, inputs from our TPA Services team, and any other reporting sources into a single coherent picture with a human-readable summary

 - Automate the recurring work: identify activities and workflows that can be automated and implement scalable automations to drive efficiency




WHO YOU ARE

 - A self-starter and rigorous thinker who builds a point of view from ambiguous data and strives to deliver actionable insights

 - Comfortable in Salesforce and fluent with data tools; continuous improvement mindset for refining existing workflows and systems

 - Comfortable using AI and automation tools as a core part of your daily work, and willing to deploy skills, automations, and workflows using AI tools

 - No traditional Sales Ops background required. Bestow values problem-solvers who challenge assumptions and find better ways to get things done

 - Takes initiative and can own a project end-to-end, and also knows when to find subject-matter experts before going too far down the wrong path




TOTAL REWARDS

At Bes

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager (Communications Team)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Engineering team is responsible for all technology for Bestow and the Bestow Platform. We serve employees, consumers, and enterprise customers. In addition to building software, we integrate and manage many third-party products used by employees and as part of the platform. Our major functional teams are product engineering, infrastructure, IT, data & analytics, and program management. Engineering works closely with product management and design to define and prioritize the product roadmap.

As an Engineering Manager for the Communications Team, you will lead a team of Backend Software Engineers responsible for Bestow's communications infrastructure, the Document Creation Service and Notification Service. You will partner with Product Managers, Compliance, Legal, and our external delivery partners to develop and support solutions that power document generation, regulatory disclosures, and multi-channel customer communications across the platform. You will also collaborate with teams across Bestow to plan and execute the product roadmap. Our system runs on Kubernetes in Google Cloud Platform and is composed of several microservices written in Go that integrate using gRPC, REST, and async messaging patterns. You will share responsibility for operating our workloads on these platforms.

A key focus for this role will be scaling our communications infrastructure, building reliable, compliant document generation and multi-channel notification delivery (email, SMS, and physical mail) that supports policy documents, required notices, automated delivery tracking, and seamless carrier and partner integrations.

This role is open to remote candidates. #LI-Remote




WHAT YOU’LL DO

This is a player/coach role with direct reports, spending approximately 20% of your time contributing production-level code.


TECHNICAL LEADERSHIP

 - Remain hands-on in the code and actively participate in system design and architecture decisions

 - Regularly review pull requests, providing thoughtful feedback that elevates code quality

 - Partner with engineers on complex technical challenges, offering guidance without micromanaging

 - Drive large initiatives from conception through delivery


COMMUNICATIONS ARCHITECTURE & INTEGRATION

 - Own the document generation pipeline including templating, PDF rendering, carrier-and product-specific document configuration, and template lifecycle management

 - Lead multi-channel notification delivery across email, SMS, and physical mail, including third-party provider integrations (e.g., Lob, SendGrid, Twilio), delivery status tracking, and webhook handling

 - Design delivery failure handling including correct classification of provider errors (e.g., distinguishing user

source_jobs.title: Product Designer II
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BESTOW

Life insurance is one of the world's most important products. It's also one of the hardest to build, distribute, and modernize. Bestow exists to change that.

Bestow is a leading vertical technology platform serving some of the largest and most innovative life insurers. Our platform unifies the fragmented, legacy value chain, enabling carriers to launch products in weeks instead of years. Carriers choose us to scale and operate at unprecedented speed, powered by AI and automation.

Bestow isn't selling policies. We're building the infrastructure that helps an entire industry move faster, reach more people, and deliver on its promise.

Backed by leading investors (Goldman Sachs, Hedosophia, NEA, Valar, 8VC) and trusted by major carriers, Bestow is powered by a team that moves with precision, purpose, and heart. If you want to help reimagine a centuries-old industry with lasting impact, join us.

Bestow offers flexible remote/hybrid work, meaningful benefits, equity, and substantial growth opportunities.

Bestow uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit E-Verify.gov http://e-verify.gov.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Bestow Product organization is responsible for defining, designing, and delivering exceptional digital experiences that solve real problems. Our team collaborates across Engineering, Revenue, and Ops to transform ideas into intuitive, valuable solutions that delight our users. We believe in evidence-based decision making and maintaining a relentless focus on customer outcomes to drive product excellence.

You will join the Underwriting module — the team shaping how underwriters and managers work inside the Bestow workbench: case management, capacity and workload, analytics, and the AI-enabled experiences that make complex insurance decisions faster and more trustworthy. This is operational, B2B product design in a regulated domain — dense workflows, high stakes, and real users who depend on clarity under pressure.

You will report to the Senior Manager, UX Design. This role is open to Remote (US). #LIRemote





ABOUT THE ROLE

Bestow is seeking a Product Designer II to join our Underwriting module's mission of making insurance more accessible and human-centered. You'll own design for key workbench initiatives — turning complex underwriting and manager workflows into experiences that are clear, efficient, and defensible.

We're looking for a designer who combines strong research instincts with the ability to shape and ship ideas quickly. The most meaningful work on this team happens where ambiguity is high: discovery starts before requirements are fully baked, and the best designers here don't wait for a finished PRD — they help define the problem, test direction, and bring partners along with evidence.

This team is prototype-first. You'll work in Figma and in coded prototypes — often in shared repos alongside engineering and product — using AI tooling to move faster without sacrificing judgment. If you're energized by the shift from static handoffs to living prototypes, and you can speak concretely to how you've adapted your workflow between canvas and code, you'll thrive here.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own end-to-end design for assigned initiatives — from problem framing and research through prototyping, validation, and implementation partnership

 - Conduct and synthesize research through user interviews, usability testing, and data analysis to validate design decisions and inform product strategy

 - Shape solutions in partnership with product managers — helping define problems, outline approaches, and establish success criteria when requirements are still forming

 - Create user flows, diagrams, Figma designs, and coded prototypes that communicate direction clearly to stakeholders and engineering

 - Navigate and collaborate in shared code repositories — reading existing prototype

notes: Life insurance tech platform with strong engineering focus
